summary.intent: Migrate the Gossamer Electron app's UI to Mantine component library, improve the sessions screen with better table UX (truncation, copy-to-clipboard, activity indicators, expandable rows), apply a GitHub-inspired dark/light theme, and fix dark mode issues across all screens.
summary.outcome: Mantine was fully integrated with a custom GitHub-inspired theme and persistent color scheme. All screens and components were migrated. Dark mode issues were fixed using the `light-dark()` CSS function. The sessions table was enhanced with truncation, copy-to-clipboard, activity dots, and row navigation. A breadcrumb context was added to the header showing username/repo/session-id.

repo: Project is at /Users/stephanostsoucas/gossamer — Electron app lives in app/, server in src/server.ts, DB schema in prisma/schema.prisma
Renderer source is at app/src/renderer/src/ with screens/ and components/ subdirectories
CheckpointSessionMetadata.createdAt was used to populate Checkpoint.createdAt in the server response since CheckpointMetadata has no createdAt field
Activity status thresholds (matching SessionTree): <2min=green (active), <15min=orange (idle), else gray (inactive), based on session updatedAt

code.finding: AppShell.Header must span full viewport width to keep hamburger visible when sidebar is collapsed — placing toggle in AppShell.Main causes it to disappear when sidebar closes
light-dark(lightValue, darkValue) CSS function is the correct pattern for theme-aware colors in Mantine v7 — static var(--mantine-color-dark-N) never switches between schemes
SessionRow returns a React fragment with two Table.Tr elements (main row + optional expanded row) — must be inside Table.Tbody in the parent
Created a React context (BreadcrumbContext) to push breadcrumb data from child screens up to the Layout header — screens set crumbs via useEffect and clear on unmount
All markdown renderer colors were hardcoded light values — code blocks, inline code, table borders, text colors all needed light-dark() replacements

workflow: Mantine v7 requires postcss.config.cjs with postcss-preset-mantine and postcss-simple-vars for CSS variable support
Mantine color variants like var(--mantine-color-X-0) are always the lightest shade regardless of color scheme — use light-dark() instead for theme-aware backgrounds
localStorageColorSchemeManager persists color scheme preference across Electron app restarts
Running electron-vite build after each change is the fastest way to catch TypeScript/import errors without launching the full app

friction: Dark mode required multiple rounds of fixes — SessionDetail, EventItem, ToolGroupItem, ToolUseItem, CheckpointMessageItem, and MarkdownView all had separate hardcoded light colors that weren't caught in the initial migration
Hamburger button placement required two iterations: first placed in AppShell.Main (hidden when sidebar collapsed), then correctly moved to AppShell.Header
Sidebar background dark mode fix required two iterations: first used static dark color token, then switched to light-dark() CSS function
User interrupted the 'add most recent checkpoint column' feature mid-implementation and changed direction to expandable rows instead
Expandable checkpoint rows were later removed entirely in favor of direct row-click navigation
open_items: CheckpointDetail.tsx and SessionTree.tsx may still have hardcoded light colors (var(--mantine-color-teal-0), var(--mantine-color-gray-0)) that weren't fixed in this session
Breadcrumb on main sessions screen uses the first/most recent session's user+repo — if sessions span multiple repos this will show incorrect context
MarkdownView code block syntax highlighting uses a static dark theme (#1e293b background) — light mode code blocks use a plain light background with no syntax highlighting adaptation
No build verification was run after the final breadcrumb changes
